source: Vacsera — Egypt National Childhood Immunization Schedule
source_notes: Schedule data sourced from Vacsera (Egypt's national vaccine manufacturer and distributor), confirmed in Nomou clinical application data (egypt-v1.0-mar-2026), clinically reviewed by Ahmed Zayed MD on 2026-03-15. Compulsory antigens are confirmed. Recommended antigens are confirmed as existing in the Egypt programme but with variable uptake.
scope: Routine childhood immunisation, birth to 72 months (6 years). Compulsory (EPI) and recommended antigens included with category distinction.
context_note: Source-country context file. In cross-border reconciliation, doses are validated against the TARGET country schedule (Germany/STIKO), not this source schedule. This file provides context for interpreting what a dose entry on an Egyptian card likely represents.
key_differences_vs_germany: Egypt uses OPV (oral); Germany uses IPV only (no OPV)
Egypt hexavalent uses DTPw (whole-cell pertussis); Germany uses DTaP (acellular pertussis) — both satisfy DTP antigen requirement
BCG at 1 month in Egypt; not in German schedule
Egypt MMR at 12m + 18m; Germany MMR at 11m + 15m
Varicella not compulsory in Egypt (recommended only); compulsory in Germany at 11m + 15m

doses.notes: Zero dose at birth (day 1 of life). Standalone HepB birth dose.
Egyptian schedule lists BCG at 1 month (30 days). WHO generally recommends at birth; the local Vacsera schedule uses 1 month.
OPV zero dose at 1 month. Egypt uses bivalent OPV (bOPV) throughout its routine schedule.

Egypt's 6-in-1 vaccine covering DPT (whole-cell pertussis), Hib, Hepatitis B, and IPV. Given at 2 months. Note: Egypt uses DTPw (whole-cell), unlike Germany's DTaP (acellular). Both satisfy the DTP antigen requirement for cross-border reconciliation.
OPV dose 1 of primary series, co-administered with Hexavalent at 2 months.
OPV dose 2 of primary series, co-administered with Hexavalent at 4 months.
Third hexavalent dose at 6 months. Completes primary series.
OPV dose 3 of primary series, co-administered with Hexavalent at 6 months.
MMR dose 1 at 12 months. Egypt transitioned from measles-monovalent to MMR; cards from children born before ~2020 may show measles-only at 9 months — that is a legacy record (see legacy_patterns).
OPV booster 1 at 12 months, co-administered with MMR dose 1.
DPT booster (Diphtheria, Pertussis, Tetanus) at 18 months. Standalone DPT booster — does not include Hib or HepB. May appear as 'Tresivac' or 'DPT' on Egyptian cards.
MMR dose 2 at 18 months. Minimum 4 weeks from dose 1.
Diphtheria-Tetanus (DT) booster at school age (4–6 years). Does not include pertussis component.
Recommended (not compulsory EPI). WHO-essential. PCV10 or PCV13. Variable uptake in public facilities; common in private practice.

Recommended only — NOT compulsory EPI in Egypt. Available privately. This is a key reconciliation gap: children from Egypt moving to Germany will typically be missing Varicella because it is not in Egypt's public EPI programme.
Recommended second dose at 24 months. Minimum 4 weeks from dose 1.

legacy_patterns.description: Cards from children vaccinated before Egypt's MMR transition (~2020) may show different patterns. The agent should recognise these as valid historical records.

patterns.maps_to: Partial MMR coverage — Measles component only. Mumps and Rubella not covered.
patterns.action: Count as Measles dose 1; flag Mumps and Rubella as unverified.

patterns.maps_to: DTaP + Hib + HepB without IPV component
patterns.action: Count DTaP/Hib/HepB as given; flag IPV as not covered by that product.

interval_rules.notes: Egypt 2-4-6 schedule; standard 8-week interval. ACIP minimum 28 days.
